description: Length-matched V2 facts for analysis and PDE conjectures domain.

facts.context: For 3D Navier-Stokes, energy inequality and weak solutions are known. The open gap is:
facts.truth: whether weak solutions are unique and remain smooth for all time
distractors: whether energy is exactly conserved in the zero viscosity limit
whether weak solutions exist at all for arbitrary initial velocity data
whether the pressure field can be uniquely recovered from velocity alone

facts.context: For 3D incompressible Euler equations, finite-time blowup:
facts.truth: is conjectured but unproven for smooth data; Elgindi showed blowup for C^(1,alpha)
distractors: has been completely ruled out by applying the Beale-Kato-Majda criterion
is proven to occur for all smooth compactly supported initial velocity data
is irrelevant since 3D Euler possesses global weak solutions for all data

facts.context: The Kakeya conjecture states that Kakeya sets (containing unit segment in each direction) have:
facts.truth: Hausdorff dimension n in R^n; proven for n=2, open for n>=3
distractors: Hausdorff dimension n-1 in R^n; fully proven in all dimensions
Hausdorff dimension (n+1)/2 achieved by Wolff bound in every case
measure zero with full Hausdorff dimension proven in all R^n spaces

facts.context: The Erdos distinct distances problem: n points in the plane determine at least:
facts.truth: cn/sqrt(log n) distinct distances, proven by Guth-Katz in 2015
distractors: cn^(2/3) distinct distances, proven by Solymosi-Toth and now settled
exactly Theta(n) distances, established by Szemeredi-Trotter completely
cn^(4/5) distinct distances, proven by Katz-Tardos as the sharp bound

facts.context: The local-global conjecture for elliptic curves concerns:
facts.truth: whether local solvability over all completions implies rational solvability
distractors: whether the Tate-Shafarevich group is always finite for every curve
whether finding rational points is always possible in polynomial time
whether the Mordell-Weil rank is bounded by an absolute constant

facts.context: The Erdos-Turan conjecture on additive bases states:
facts.truth: if the sum of reciprocals 1/a_n diverges, the sequence contains arbitrarily long APs
distractors: if the sum of reciprocals 1/a_n converges, the sequence must be a Sidon set
if the sum of reciprocals 1/a_n diverges, it has positive upper Banach density
if the sum of reciprocals 1/a_n diverges, it forms asymptotic basis of order 2

facts.context: The corners theorem (Ajtai-Szemeredi) proves that dense subsets of [n]^2 contain:
facts.truth: corners: triples (x,y), (x+d,y), (x,y+d) for some positive integer d
distractors: squares: quadruples (x,y), (x+d,y), (x,y+d), (x+d,y+d) for some d
diagonal lines: triples (x,y), (x+d,y+d), (x+2d,y+2d) for some d
rectangles: quadruples with sides a,b>0 at position (x,y) in the grid

facts.context: The Sarnak conjecture on the Mobius function states that:
facts.truth: mu(n) is orthogonal to all deterministic (zero topological entropy) sequences
distractors: mu(n) correlates strongly with the Liouville function over all intervals
mu(n) has uniformly bounded partial sums along arithmetic progressions
mu(n) is equidistributed among its three values with equal frequency

facts.context: Singmaster's conjecture states that how many times a number >1 appears in Pascal's triangle is:
facts.truth: bounded; conjectured at most 8, with known repeated entries including 120
distractors: unbounded; proven by Luca that C(2n,n) entries appear arbitrarily often
exactly 4 for all sufficiently large entries, as proven by Kane in 2007
at most logarithmic in n, following from exponential binomial coefficient growth

facts.context: The Furstenberg conjecture on x2, x3 ergodicity states:
facts.truth: the only jointly ergodic x2 and x3 invariant measure on R/Z is Lebesgue measure
distractors: every x2-invariant probability measure is automatically also x3-invariant on R/Z
the only ergodic measure for x2 alone on the circle R/Z is the Dirac mass at 0
there exist uncountably many distinct jointly ergodic x2 and x3 invariant measures

facts.context: For minimal surfaces in R^n, interior regularity holds for n<=7. For n>=8:
facts.truth: singular minimal hypersurfaces exist, shown by Bombieri-De Giorgi-Giusti in 1969
distractors: all minimal hypersurfaces remain smooth everywhere by Schoen-Simon regularity
the singular set has codimension at most 2 in all dimensions by Federer theorem
the singular set has Hausdorff dimension exactly n-8 for every minimal surface

facts.context: Uniform boundedness of partial sums of Fourier series for C^1 functions:
facts.truth: is proven; the question remains open for Holder-continuous C^alpha with alpha<1
distractors: is proven for all continuous functions as a consequence of Carleson's theorem
fails even for C^1 functions, with du Bois-Reymond counterexample applying
is equivalent to the Riemann hypothesis for general Dirichlet L-function series
